semantic.role: Service-level configuration resolver for execution profiles, mapping resolved LLM settings to a structured generation strategy profile.
responsibilities: Define the `ResolvedExecutionProfile` interface that encapsulates the generation strategy and its source of truth.
Define the `RuntimeCommand` union type representing commands supported by the runtime (sync, check, fix).
Export `resolveExecutionProfileFromSettings` function that transforms an external `ResolvedLLMSettings` object into a `ResolvedExecutionProfile` for downstream runtime consumption.
outOfScope: Actual execution of runtime commands (sync, check, fix) – this is about configuration resolution only.
LLM provider instantiation or credential management.
Persistence or caching of execution profiles.
invariants: Must always produce a valid `ResolvedExecutionProfile` when given a fully resolved `ResolvedLLMSettings`.
The `generationStrategy` in the output must be a valid `GenerationStrategy` from the prompt-policy module.
The function must not have side effects (pure transformation).

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Provide a thin, focused configuration resolver that decouples LLM settings from runtime execution profiles, enabling runtime services to make decisions based on a resolved profile.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: No changes specific to this file; the repository-wide commit message indicates general release gate fixes and source synchronization.

publicSurface.description: Interface defining an execution profile with a generation strategy and its source identifier. Used by runtime services to determine how to generate responses.
Union type ('sync' | 'check' | 'fix') representing the currently selected runtime command. Consumed by orchestrators to branch execution flow.
Pure function that maps a ResolvedLLMSettings object to a ResolvedExecutionProfile. Called by runtime initialization to derive the active generation strategy.
